description_long: This object consists of two croissants placed side by side, each with a golden-brown, slightly matte exterior and a plump, crescent shape. The surface displays subtle color gradients from light tan to deeper golden hues, with gentle ridges and folds characteristic of laminated pastry dough. The croissants have a soft, pillowy appearance, with some visible layering along the curved edges, suggesting a tender, airy interior. There are no visible toppings or fillings, and the surface is free from any glaze or seeds. The overall form is irregular, with one croissant slightly larger and more rounded than the other, both exhibiting the classic French pastry look. The texture appears semi-soft, with a slightly rough, matte finish typical of freshly baked croissants.
description: Two golden-brown, crescent-shaped croissants with a matte, slightly rough surface and visible layered folds. They appear soft, airy, and free of toppings or glaze.
description_view_0: A front-side view showing both croissants, highlighting their rounded, plump bodies and the gentle layering along the edges.
description_view_1: A top-down view emphasizing the broad, crescent shape and the subtle color variations across the surface.
description_view_2: A side view that accentuates the height and curvature of the croissants, with visible folds and a slightly pointed tip on one.
description_view_3: A rear view showing the back curves and the more compact, rounded ends of both croissants.
